text.title: Client Firing Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Client Firing Protocol` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The ADR MUST cite the specific criteria triggered (payment, scope abuse, burn-out, ICP) with evidence links. Vibes-only ADRs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Vibes-only firings reopen as regret months later; explicit criteria force a clean rationale.

rule.statement: The ADR MUST link to a transition plan (handoff docs, asset inventory, referral alternative, comms schedule). 'Will figure out' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Un-planned transitions cause 60+ days of follow-up; an attached plan kills the tail.

rule.statement: The ADR MUST cite the contract clause invoked and the notice period applied. Same-day exits without a safety/harassment trigger are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Same-day exits routinely escalate to legal; the cited clause keeps the exit defensible.

rule.statement: The ADR MUST tag itself one-way-door (cannot re-engage) OR two-way-door (re-engage possible after specific repair). Default tag is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Untagged decisions get re-opened impulsively; explicit class enforces the post-decision discipline.

rule.statement: Within 14 days of termination, founder MUST record an ICP / intake-form update that would have caught this client at intake. Skipped refinement guarantees a clone next quarter.
rule.rationale: Without refinement, intake stays leaky and the same bad-fit profile re-enters; 14-day window forces extraction.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
